24.reason: Logical error: Incorrect and non-portable condition for filtering non-alphabetic characters. Should use !isalpha(tp) from <ctype.h>.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Logical error: Incorrect and non-portable condition for filtering non-alphabetic characters. Should use !isalpha(tp) from <ctype.h>.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.125 , reason:  Logical error: condition for skipping non-letters is flawed for certain ASCII characters (e.g., symbols between 'Z' and 'a'); using !isalpha(tp) is safer and standard.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.125 , reason:  [This condition incorrectly includes non-alphabetic characters between 'Z' and 'a' (like '[', '\', etc.) as valid separators, but also fails to properly detect alphabetic characters due to flawed logic. It should use isalpha() instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [This condition incorrectly includes non-alphabetic characters between 'Z' and 'a' like '[', '\', etc., as valid separators, but also fails to properly detect alphabetic characters due to flawed logic. It should use isalpha() instead.]

77.reason: Logical error: p is never updated to point to the tail of the list; it remains pointing to head. This overwrites head->next instead of appending the new node to the end.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Logical error: p is never updated to point to the tail of the list; it remains pointing to head. This overwrites head->next instead of appending the new node to the end.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.125 , reason:  Logical error: p is not updated to point to the tail node when a duplicate word is found (same==1), causing incorrect list linkage and potential data loss.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.125 , reason:  [p is uninitialized when head != NULL and same==0 on first insertion after head. This leads to writing to random memory.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Here, 'p' is used uninitialized when head != NULL but no new node has been assigned to p yet. In the first insertion, p is set, but in subsequent insertions, p may not point to the last node, leading to undefined behavior or segmentation fault.]

97.reason: Logical error: Inner loop bound for bubble sort is incorrect. It should be j < g - i - 1, otherwise p becomes NULL and accessing p->w causes a segmentation fault.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Logical error: Inner loop bound for bubble sort is incorrect. It should be j < g - i - 1, otherwise p becomes NULL and accessing p->w causes a segmentation fault.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.125 , reason:  Compilation error in C89/C90. Logical: loop bound does not account for p becoming NULL, leading to segmentation fault in strcmp(q->w,p->w).
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.125 , reason:  [When i=0 and j starts, if g>=1, then p = head->next. But if the list has only one node, p is NULL, leading to dereferencing NULL in strcmp(q->w,p->w).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [When j reaches the last iteration, p may become NULL, and then strcmp(q->w, p->w) will dereference a NULL pointer, causing a runtime crash. The loop bound should be g-i-1 to avoid accessing beyond the list.]

101.reason: Logical error: t is initialized to q, making this condition always true at the start of the loop. It fails to correctly identify if q is the head node for swapping. Should check if (q == head).

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.125 , reason:  [This condition attempts to check if t == q (i.e., swapping head), but comparing strings instead of pointers is incorrect and unreliable.]
